historicalEvidence.failureClass: identical binding-scope content produced different verdicts after pull_request context disappeared

rows.reason: The old resolver returned a lenient verdict when PR-only environment existed and capture objects were unavailable.
rows.remediation: Use a single scoped-tree-content comparison path, require full history, and make event variables irrelevant to the verdict.

rows.reason: captureCommitSha was interpreted as current commit identity in fallback paths.
rows.remediation: Declare bindingMode=scoped-tree-content-v1; the SHA now identifies the source snapshot whose scoped blobs are compared to the worktree.

rows.reason: This is the explicit artifact producer. It records the source snapshot and scoped-content binding mode; it does not decide PR/main pass or fail.
PR context used pull_request.base.sha while push context could compare HEAD to origin/main=HEAD; short prerequisite commit IDs also coupled the verdict to history identity.
rows.remediation: Resolve a non-self merge-stable base and verify #709/#711 semantic surface markers at both that base and HEAD.

rows.reason: The push fallback could pass origin/main=HEAD to the protected-runtime diff and silently evaluate an empty range.
rows.remediation: Delegate base selection to the non-self merge-stable resolver and fail closed when no prior boundary exists.

rows.reason: The first resolvable candidate was accepted even when its merge-base was HEAD.
rows.remediation: Reject self merge-bases and fall back to HEAD^1, preserving the same pre-change boundary in PR and push contexts.

rows.reason: All environment candidates flow through one rule: a merge-base equal to HEAD is rejected, then HEAD^1 is used as a context-neutral fallback.
The core evaluates current-tree lane coverage, topology and the RPC validator; commit/run IDs are diagnostics and artifact provenance, not a lenient verdict branch.
The mandatory wrapper runs the audit and run-twice parity fixture before delegating to the existing topology wrapper/core.
It runs on pull_request and push/main. PR SHAs only scope changed-test planning; push evaluates FULL, and verify-pack fetches full history for content binding.
A pull_request_target trusted-base admission guard has no post-merge action to perform; current-tree policy is independently exercised by scope-guard.
This privileged workflow monitors and merges one named bot PR. A push/main sibling would have no pull request to authenticate or merge.
Runtime history is a post-merge/scheduled measurement producer; PR-required correctness remains in scope-guard.
Reusable workflow_call surface; the caller owns event membership.
The same check is present on pull_request and push/main, with workflow_dispatch for operator runs.
The same policy check is present on pull_request and push/main.
Both event types run the foundation suite; baseline resolution now rejects origin/main=HEAD for push jobs.
